title: Accretion (astrophysics)
body: In astrophysics, accretion is the accumulation of particles into a massive object by gravitationally attracting more matter, typically gaseous matter, into an accretion disk. Most astronomical objects, such as galaxies, stars, and planets, are formed by accretion processes.
categories: All articles with specifically marked weasel-worded phrases
